statement: Higher weekly resistance-training set volume is associated with greater hypertrophy across a low-to-moderate range, with evidence of a dose-response relationship.
evidence_note: The range is an approximate evidence-supported programming range, not an exact validated threshold.

statement: Very low weekly volume can still increase hypertrophy, but tends to produce smaller hypertrophy gains than moderate weekly volume.
In trained men, higher weekly set volumes can enhance hypertrophy more than lower volumes, but strength improvements may not increase proportionally.
evidence_note: RCT evidence in trained men supports hypertrophy benefits from higher volume, but results vary by muscle and design.

statement: Multiple sets per exercise generally produce greater strength gains than a single set, but strength gains show diminishing returns as set volume rises.
evidence_note: This evidence is expressed per session. Rules using weekly volume should treat it as directional support unless a weekly strength-volume range is added in a future version.

statement: At high weekly volumes, hypertrophy benefits may plateau or become less predictable, indicating diminishing returns and possible recovery constraints.
evidence_note: Exact upper limits vary by person, muscle group, proximity to failure, exercise selection, and recovery capacity.

statement: Novices can gain strength with lower resistance-training volume than more trained lifters, although multiple sets remain generally superior to single sets.
More trained individuals may require higher or more carefully managed volume than novices to continue hypertrophy progress.
evidence_note: Evidence supports training-age differences directionally, but exact advanced-lifter volume requirements are not firmly quantified.

statement: Weekly volume is often a more useful volume target than per-session volume because the same weekly volume can be distributed across sessions.
evidence_note: Frequency-specific recommendations belong to phase1b.frequency; this statement only supports tracking weekly volume.

statement: MEV, MAV, and MRV are practitioner volume-landmark constructs rather than thresholds directly validated by meta-analysis.
evidence_note: Stored only as a flagged practitioner framework because the verified literature supports dose-response relationships and approximate ranges, not validated MEV, MAV, or MRV thresholds.
